author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter V. 19.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The words of our Lord Jesus Christ, especially those recorded by the Evangelist John,—who not without cause leaned on the Lord’s bosom, that he might drink in the secrets of that higher wisdom, and by evangelizing give forth again what by loving he had drunk in,—are so secret and profound of understanding, that they trouble all who are perverse of heart, and exercise all who are in heart upright.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, beloved, give heed to these few words that have been read.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see if in any wise we can, by His own gift and help who has willed His words to be recited to us, which at that time were heard and committed to writing that they might now be read, what He means in what ye have now heard Him say: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing: for what things soever the Father doeth, these same the Son also doeth in like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now you need to be reminded whence this discourse arose, by reason of what precedes this passage, where the Lord had cured a certain man among those who were lying in the five porches of that pool of Solomon, and to whom He had said, “Take up thy bed, and go unto thy house.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this He had done on the Sabbath; and hence the Jews, being troubled, were falsely accusing Him as a destroyer and transgressor of the law.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He then said to them, “My Father worketh even until now, and I work.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they, taking the observance of the Sabbath in a carnal sense, fancied that God had, as it were, slept after the labor of framing the world even to this day; and that therefore He had sanctified that day, from which He began to rest as from labor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, to our fathers of old there was ordained a sacrament of the Sabbath, which we Christians observe spiritually, in abstaining from every servile work, that is, from every sin (for the Lord saith, “Every one that committeth sin is the servant of sin”), and in having rest in our heart, that is, spiritual tranquillity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And although in this life we strive after this rest, yet not until we have departed this life shall we attain to that perfect rest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the reason why God is said to have rested is, that He made no creature after all was finished.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, the Scripture called it rest, to admonish us that after good works we shall rest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thus we have it written in Genesis, “And God made all things very good, and God rested on the seventh day,” in order that thou, O man, considering that God Himself is said to have rested after good works, shouldest not expect rest for thyself, until after thou hast wrought good works; and even as God after He made man in His own image and likeness, and in him finished all His works very good, rested on the seventh day, so mayest thou also not expect rest to thyself, except thou return to that likeness in which thou wast made, which likeness thou hast lost by sinning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, in reality, God cannot be said to have toiled, who “said, and they were done.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is there that, after such facility of work, desires to rest as if after labor?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He commanded and some one resisted Him, if He commanded and it was not done, and labored that it might be done, then justly He should be said to have rested after labor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when in that same book of Genesis we read, “God said, Let there be light, and there was light; God said, Let there be a firmament, and the firmament was made, and all the rest were made immediately at His word: to which also the psalm testifies, saying, “He spake, and they were made; He commanded, and they were created,”—how could He require rest after the world was made, as if to enjoy leisure after toil, He who in commanding never toiled?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consequently these sayings are mystical, and are laid down in this wise that we may be looking for rest after this life, provided we have done good works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, the Lord, restraining the impudence and refuting the error of the Jews, and showing them that they did not think rightly of God, says to them, when they were offended at His working men’s healing on the Sabbath, “My Father worketh until now, and I work:” do not therefore suppose that my Father so rested on the Sabbath, that thenceforth He doth not work; but even as He now worketh, so I also work.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as the Father without toil, so too the Son without toil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God “said, and they were done;” Christ said to the impotent man, “Take up thy bed, and go unto thy house,” and it was done.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the catholic faith has it, that the works of the Father and of the Son are not separable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is what I wish, if possible, to speak to you, beloved; but, according to those words of the Lord, “he that is able to receive it, let him receive it.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he that is not able to receive it, let him not charge it on me, but on his own dullness; and let him turn to Him that opens the heart, that He may pour in what He freely giveth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, lastly, if any one may not have understood, because I have not declared it as I ought to have declared it, let him excuse the weakness of man, and supplicate the divine goodness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we have within a Master, Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whatever ye are not able to receive through your ear and my mouth, turn ye in your heart to Him who both teacheth me what to speak, and distributeth to you in what measure He deigns.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who knows what to give, and to whom to give, will help him that seeketh, and open to him that knocketh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if so be that He give not, let no one call himself forsaken.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it may be that He delays to give something, but He leaves none hungry.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, indeed, He give not at the hour, He is exercising the seeker, He is not scorning the suitor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Look ye, then, and give heed to what I wish to say, even if I should not be able to say it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The catholic faith, confirmed by the Spirit of God in His saints, has this against all heretical perverseness, that the works of the Father and of the Son are inseparable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this that I have said?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As the Father and the Son are inseparable, so also the works of the Father and of the Son are inseparable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How are the Father and the Son inseparable, since Himself said, “I and the Father are one?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because the Father and the Son are not two Gods, but one God, the Word and He whose the Word is, One and the Only One, Father and Son bound together by charity, One God, and the Spirit of Charity also one, so that Father, Son, and Holy Spirit is made the Trinity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, not only of the Father and Son, but also of the Holy Spirit; as there is equality and inseparability of persons, so also the works are inseparable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I will tell you yet more plainly what is meant by “the works are inseparable.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The catholic faith does not say that God the Father made something, and the Son made some other thing; but what the Father made, that also the Son made, that also the Holy Spirit made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For all things were made by the Word; when “He spoke and they were done,” it is by the Word they were done, by Christ they were done.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God: all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If all things were made by Him, “God said, Let there be light, and there was light; in the Word He made, by the Word He made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, then, we have now heard the Gospel, where He answered the Jews who were indignant “that He not only broke the Sabbath, but said also that God was His Father, making Himself equal with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For so it is written in the foregoing paragraph.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, therefore, the Son of God, the Truth, made answer to their erring indignation, saith He, “Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing;” as if He said, “Why are ye offended because I have said that God is my Father, and that I make myself equal with God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am equal in that wise that He begat me; I am equal in that wise that He is not from me, but I from Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is implied in these words: “The Son cannot do anything of Himself, but what He seeth the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is, whatever the Son hath to do, the doing it He hath of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why of the Father hath He the doing it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because of the Father He hath it that He is Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why hath He it of the Father to be Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because of the Father He hath it that He is able, of the Father that He is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, to the Son, both to be able and to be is the self-same thing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not so with man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Raise your hearts by all means from a comparison of human weakness, that lies far beneath; and should any of us perhaps reach to the secret, and, while awe-struck by the brilliance as it were of a great light, should discern somewhat, and not remain wholly ignorant; yet let him not imagine that he understands the whole, lest he should become proud, and lose what knowledge he has gotten.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With man, to be and to be able are different things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For sometimes the man is, and yet cannot what he wills; sometimes, again, the man is in such wise, that he can what he wills; therefore his being and his being able are different things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if man’s esse and posse were the same thing, then he could when he would.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But with God it is not so, that His substance to be is one thing, and His power to be able another thing; but whatever is His, and whatever He is, is consubstantial with Him, because He is God: it is not so that in one way He is, in another way is able; He has the esse and the posse together, because He has to will and to do together.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, then, the power of the Son is of the Father, therefore also the substance of the Son is of the Father; and since the substance of the Son is of the Father, therefore the power of the Son is of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the Son, power and substance are not different: the power is the self-same that the substance is; the substance to be, the power to be able.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, because the Son is of the Father, He said, “The Son cannot of Himself do anything.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because He is not Son from Himself, therefore He is not able from Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He appears to have made Himself as it were less, when He said, “The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hereupon heretical vanity lifts the neck; theirs, indeed, who say that the Son is less than the Father, of less authority, of less majesty, of less possibility, not understanding the mystery of Christ’s words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But attend, beloved, and see how they are confounded in their carnal intellect by the words of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this is what I said a little before, that the word of God troubles all perverse hearts, just as it exercises pious hearts, especially that spoken by the Evangelist John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they are deep words that are spoken by him, not random words, nor such as may be easily understood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, a heretic, if he happen to hear these words, immediately rises and says to us, “Lo, the Son is less than the Father; hear the words of the Son, who says, ‘The Son cannot do anything of Himself, but what He seeth the Father doing.’”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wait; as it is written, “Be meek to hear the word, that thou mayest understand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, suppose that because I assert the power and majesty of the Father and of the Son to be equal, I was disconcerted at hearing these words, “The Son cannot do anything of Himself, but what He seeth the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, I, being disconcerted at these words, will ask thee, who seemest to thyself to have instantly understood them, a question.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We know in the Gospel that the Son walked upon the sea; when saw He the Father walk upon the sea?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here now he is disconcerted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lay aside, then, thy understanding of the words, and let us examine them together.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What do we then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have heard the words of the Lord: “The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son walked upon the sea, the Father never walked upon the sea.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet certainly “the Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Return then with me to what I was saying, in case it is so to be understood that we may both escape from the question.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I see how I, according to the catholic faith, may escape without tripping or stumbling; whilst thou, on the other hand, shut in on every side, art seeking a way of escape.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See by what way thou hast entered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps thou hast not understood this that I said, See by what way thou hast entered: hear Himself saying, “I am the door.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not without cause, then, art thou seeking how thou mayest get out; and this only thou findest, that thou hast not entered by the door, but fell in over the wall.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore raise thyself up from thy fall how thou canst, and enter by the door, that thou mayest go in without stumbling, and go out without straying.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come by Christ, not bringing forward of thy own heart what thou mayest say; but what He shows, that speak.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold how the catholic faith gets clear of this question.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son walked upon the sea, planted the feet of flesh on the waves: the flesh walked, and the divinity directed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when the flesh was walking and the divinity directing, was the Father absent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If absent, how doth the Son Himself say, “but the Father abiding in me, Himself doeth the works?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the Father, abiding in the Son, Himself doeth His works, then that walking upon the sea was made by the Father, and through the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, that walking is an inseparable work of Father and Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I see both acting in it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Neither the Father forsook the Son, nor the Son left the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, whatever the Son doeth, He doeth not without the Father; because whatever the Father doeth, He doeth not without the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have got clear of this question.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Mark ye that rightly we say the works of the Father, of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit are inseparable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as thou understandest it, lo, God made the light, and the Son saw the Father making light, according to thy carnal understanding, who wilt have it that He is less, because He said, “The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God the Father made light; what other light did the Son make?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God the Father made the firmament, the heaven between waters and waters; and the Son saw Him, according to thy dull and sluggish understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, since the Son saw the Father making the firmament, and also said, “The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing,” then show me the other firmament made by the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou lost the foundation?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they that are “built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ Himself being the chief corner-stone,” are brought into a state of peace in Christ; nor do they strive and wander in heresy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore we understand that the light was made by God the Father, but through the Son; that the firmament was made by God the Father, but through the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “all things were made through Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Cast out thine understanding, which ought not to be called understanding, but evidently foolishness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God the Father made the world; what other world did the Son make?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Show me the Son’s world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whose is this world in which we are?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Tell us, by whom made?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou sayest, “By the Son, not by the Father,” then thou hast erred from the Father; if thou sayest, “By the Father, not by the Son,” the Gospel answers thee thus, “And the world was made by (through) Him, and the world knew Him not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Acknowledge Him, then, by whom the world was made, and be not among those who knew not Him that made the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore the works of the Father and of the Son are inseparable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, this, “The Son cannot do anything of Himself,” would mean the same thing as if He were to say, “The Son is not from Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He is a Son, He was begotten; if begotten, He is from Him of whom He is begotten.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless, the Father begat Him equal to Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor was aught wanting to Him that begat; He who begat a co-eternal required not time to beget: who produced the Word of Himself, required not a mother to beget by; the Father begetting did not precede the Son in age, so that He should beget a Son younger than Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps some one may say, that after many ages God begat a Son in His old age.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even as the Father is without age, so the Son is without growth; neither has the one grown old nor the other increased, but equal begat equal, eternal begat eternal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, says some one, has eternal begat eternal?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As a temporary flame generates a temporary light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The generating flame is coeval with the light which it generates: the generating flame does not precede in time the generated light; but from the moment the flame begins, from that moment the light begins.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Show me flame without light, and I show thee God the Father without Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, “the Son cannot do anything of Himself, but what He seeth the Father doing,” implies, that for the Son to see and to be begotten of the Father, is the same thing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His seeing and His substance are not different; nor are His power and substance different.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All that He is, He is of the Father; all that He can is of the Father; because what He can and what He is is one thing, and all of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, He goes on in His own words, and troubles those that understand the matter amiss, in order to recall the erring to a right apprehension of it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After He had said, “The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing;” lest a carnal understanding of the matter should by chance creep in and turn the mind aside, and a man should imagine as it were two mechanics, one a master, the other a learner, attentively observing the master while making, say a chest, so that, as the master made the chest, the learner should make another chest according to the appearance which he looked upon while the master wrought; lest, I say, the carnal mind should frame to itself any such twofold notion in the case of the divine unity, going on, He saith, “For what things soever the Father doeth, these same also the Son doeth in like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not, the Father doeth some, the Son others like them, but the same in like manner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He saith not, What things soever the Father doeth, the Son also doeth others the like; but saith He, “What things soever the Father doeth, these same also the Son doeth in like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What things the Father doeth, these also the Son doeth: the Father made the world, the Son made the world, the Holy Ghost made the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If three Gods, then three worlds; if one God, the Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost, then one world was made by the Father, through the Son, in the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consequently the Son doeth those things which also the Father doeth, and doeth not in a different manner; He both doeth these, and doeth them in like manner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After He had said, “these doeth,” why did He add, “in like manner doeth”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lest another distorted understanding or error should spring up in the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou seest, for instance, a man’s work: in man there is mind and body; the mind rules the body, but there is a great difference between body and mind: the body is visible, the mind is invisible: there is a great difference between the power and virtue of the mind and that of any kind of body whatever, be it even a heavenly body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Still the mind rules its own body, and the body doeth; and what the mind appears to do, this the body doeth also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus the body appears to do this same thing that the mind doeth, but not “in like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How doeth this same, but not in like manner?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The mind frames a word in itself; it commands the tongue, and the tongue produces the word which the mind framed: the mind made, and the tongue made; the lord of the body made, and the servant made; but that the servant might make, it received of its lord what to make, and made while the lord commanded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The same thing was made by both, but was it in like manner?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How not in like manner? says some one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, the word that my mind formed, remains in me; that which my tongue made, passed through the smitten air, and is not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thou hast said a word in thy mind, and uttered it by thy tongue, return to thy mind, and see that the word which thou hast made is there still.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Has it remained on thy tongue, just as it has in thy mind?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was uttered by the tongue, the tongue made by sounding, the mind made by thinking; but what the tongue uttered has passed away, what the mind thought remains.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the body made that which the mind made, but not in like manner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the mind, indeed, made that which the mind may hold, but the tongue made what sounds and strikes the ear through the air.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou chase the syllables, and cause them to remain?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, not in such manner the Father and the Son; but “these same doeth,” and “in like manner doeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If God made heaven that remains, this heaven that remains the Son made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If God the Father made man that is mortal, the same man that is mortal the Son made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What things soever the Father made that endure, these things that endure made also the Son, because in like manner He made; and what things soever the Father made that are temporal, these same things that are temporal made also the Son, because He made not only the same, but also in like manner made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Father made by the Son, since by the Word the Father made all things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Seek in the Father and Son a separation, thou findest none; no, not if thou hast mounted high; no, not even if thou hast reached something above thy mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if thou turnest about among the things which thy wandering mind makes for itself, thou talkest with thine own imaginations, not with the Word of God; thine own imaginations deceive thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Mount also beyond the body, and understand the mind; mount also beyond the mind, and understand God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou reachest not unto God, unless thou hast passed beyond the mind; how much less thou reachest unto God, if thou hast tarried in the flesh!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who think of the flesh, how far are they from understanding what God is!—since they would not be there even if they knew the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Man recedes far from God when his thoughts are of the flesh; and there is a great difference between flesh and mind, yet a greater between mind and God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou art occupied with the mind, thou art in the midway: if thou directest thy attention beneath, there is the body; if above, there is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lift thyself up from the body, pass beyond even thyself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For observe what said the psalm, and thou art admonished how God must be thought of: “My tears,” it saith, “were made to me my bread day and night, when it was said to me daily, Where is thy God?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As the pagans may say, “Behold our gods, where is your God?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They indeed show us what is seen; we worship what is not seen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And to whom can we show?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To a man who has not sight with which to see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For anyhow, if they see their gods with their eyes, we too have other eyes with which to see our God: for “blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, when he had said that he was troubled, when it was daily said to him, “Where is thy God?” “these things I remembered,” saith he, “because it is daily said to me, Where is thy God?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And as if wishing to lay hold of his God, “These things,” saith he, “I remembered, and poured out my soul above me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, that I might reach unto my God, of whom it was said to me, “Where is thy God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I poured out my soul,” not over my flesh, but “above me;” I transcended myself, that I might reach unto Him: for He is above me who made me; none reaches to Him but he that passes beyond himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider the body: it is mortal, earthy, weak, corruptible; away with it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yes, perhaps thou sayest, but the body is temporal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Think then of other bodies, the heavenly; they are greater, better, more magnificent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Look at them, moreover, attentively.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They roll from east to west, they stand not; they are seen with the eyes, not only by man, but even by the beast of the field.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Pass beyond them too.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how, sayest thou, pass beyond the heavenly bodies, seeing that I walk on the earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not in the flesh dost thou pass beyond them, but in the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Away with them too: though they shine ever so much, they are bodies; though they glitter from heaven, they are bodies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come, now that perhaps thou thinkest thou hast not whither to go, after considering all these.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whither am I to go, sayest thou, beyond the heavenly bodies; and what am I to pass beyond with the mind?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou considered all these?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have, sayest thou.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By what means hast thou considered them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the being that considers appear in person.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The being that considers all these, that discriminates, distinguishes, and in a manner weighs them in the balance of wisdom, is really the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Doubtless, then, better is the mind with which thou hast contemplated all these things, than these things which thou hast contemplated.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This mind, then, is a spirit, not a body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Pass beyond it too.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that thou mayest see whither thou art to pass beyond, compare that mind itself, in the first place, with the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Heaven forbid that thou shouldest deign so to compare it!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Compare it with the brightness of the sun, of the moon, and of the stars; the brightness of the mind is greater.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Observe, first, the swiftness of the mind; see whether the scintillation of the thinking mind be not more impetuous than the brilliance of the shining sun.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With the mind thou seest the sun rising.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How slow is its motion compared with thy mind!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What the sun is about to do, thou canst think in a trice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is about to come from the east to the west; to-morrow rises from another quarter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where thy thought has done this, the sun still lags behind, and thou hast traversed the whole journey.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A great thing, therefore, is the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how do I say is?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Pass beyond it also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the mind, notwithstanding it be better than every kind of body, is itself changeable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now it knows, now knows not; now forgets, now remembers; now wills, now wills not; now errs, now is right.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Pass therefore beyond all changeableness; not only beyond all that is seen, but also beyond all that changes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thou hast passed beyond the flesh which is seen; beyond heaven, the sun, moon, and stars, which are seen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Pass, too, beyond all that changes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when thou hadst done with those things that are seen, and hadst come to thy mind, there thou didst find the changeableness of thy mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is God at all changeable?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Pass then, beyond even thy mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Pour out thy soul “above thee,” that thou mayest reach unto God, of whom it is said to thee, “Where is thy God?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not imagine that thou art to do something beyond a man’s ability.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Evangelist John himself did this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He soared beyond the flesh, beyond the earth which he trod, beyond the seas which he looked upon, beyond the air in which the fowls fly, beyond the sun, the moon, the stars, beyond all the spirits unseen, beyond his own mind, by the very reason of his rational soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Soaring beyond all these, pouring out his soul above him, whither did he arrive?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did he see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, therefore, thou seest no separation in the light, why seekest thou a separation in the work?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See God, see His Word inhering to the Word speaking, that the speaker speaks not by syllables, but this his speaking is a shining out in the brightness of wisdom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is said of the Wisdom itself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “It is the radiance of eternal light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Observe the radiance of the sun.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The sun is in the heaven, and spreads out its brightness over all lands and over all seas, and it is simply a corporal light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, indeed, thou canst separate the brightness from the sun, then separate the Word from the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am speaking of the sun.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One small, slender flame of a lamp, which can be extinguished by one breath, spreads its light over all that lies near it: thou seest the light generated by the flame spread out; thou seest its emission, but not a separation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understand, then, beloved brethren, that the Father, and the Son, and the Holy Ghost are inseparably united in themselves; that this Trinity is one God; that all the works of the one God are the works of the Father, of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All the rest which follows, and which refers to the discourse of our Lord Jesus Christ, now that a discourse is due to you to-morrow also, be present that ye may hear.
